headline: Oracle Cloud Jumps 44%, Beating Estimates and Lifting Full-Year Guidance
summary: Oracle posted Q3 adjusted EPS of $1.79, topping the $1.70 consensus, with cloud infrastructure revenue growing 44% year-over-year. The results put to rest concerns that AI was cannibalizing Oracle's core software business rather than expanding it. Raised full-year guidance signals management confidence that cloud momentum is durable, not a one-quarter spike.
oci_implication: Strong cloud growth validates OCI's infrastructure investment thesis and gives the product org a tailwind to accelerate enterprise deals. Karan's team should expect heightened customer and partner interest — now is the moment to push product-led growth initiatives and close pipeline that has been sitting on the fence.
